course_title: International Law-making in a Global World

learning_outcomes.description: Analyse current issues and trends in international law-making.
Analyse and research key concepts and issues in international law.
Negotiate and draft international legal instruments.

assessments.due_date:
21/09/2026

Further information about this assessment item, including the date and time, will be discussed in class and announced on the course website. Meantime, students should expect, at a minimum, to be available during the usual seminar time
assessments.task_description: The class will be given a draft treaty containing gaps where treaty provisions are to be negotiated. Working in groups of ~3, they will represent particular states in a mock treaty negotiation on the wording to fill those gaps. Each group will prepare and present draft clauses from the position of its state. The group will be assessed, as a whole, on the quality of their presentations.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description: In this assessment item, students will individually reflect on process and outcome of the group work.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Reflections should be submitted via BlackBoard through the usual procedure.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description: The End-of-Semester Examination will consist of two problem or essay questions focusing on Seminars 6 to 10 and associated learning materials and exercises.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 2 To Week 13(03 Aug - 01 Nov)
learning_activities.topic:
Seminars

The course will cover the following topics:

  • Week 2 - Introduction
  • Week 3 - Customary international law-making
  • Week 4 - Treaties as a source of international law: Part I
  • Week 5 - Treaties as a source of international law: Part II
  • Week 6 - Treaties as a source of international law: Part III
  • Week 7 - Other sources of international law
  • Week 8 - Strategic litigation in international law
  • Week 9 - Mock treaty negotiation (assessment)
  • Week 10 - Public Holiday
  • Week 11 - Soft & informal international law-making 
  • Week 12 - Assessing international law-making
  • Week 13 - Conclusion

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03
